title: Three nines — how much downtime?
intro: A year has 525,600 minutes. Your SLA promises 99.9% availability ('three nines').
prompt: Roughly how many minutes of downtime per year does 99.9% availability allow?
debrief: Downtime = (1 − 0.999) × 525,600 = 0.001 × 525,600 = **525.6 minutes/year** (~8.76 hours). Each extra nine cuts this ~10×: 99.99% allows ~52.6 min, 99.999% only ~5.26 min. The jump from three to five nines is a huge increase in cost and complexity — set the target from the business cost of downtime, not vanity.
